title: campanula rotundifolia var. kahenae
intro: Campanula rotundifolia var. kahenae, also known as alpine bellflower, is a rare and endemic plant species found in Hawaii. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The alpine bellflower is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, due to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. In terms of herbal preparations, it is typically dried and used in teas or infusions, with some sources suggesting it can be used topically to accelerate healing and soothe skin irritations.
